titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Laura McPhee River of No Return Collection
McPhee (Laura) River of No Return Collection
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 2, 2018 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The River of No Return Collection consists of photographs created by Laura McPhee that document sites and persons in the Sawtooth Valley of Idaho, 2003-2008, related to her work as the initial Artist in Residence in the area underwritten by the Alturas Foundation, as well as project files, 2003-2014, which contain material related imagery and products from the project, including a solo exhibition at the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, in 2006, and her book, River of No Return (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 2009). Photographic material in the collection includes color and black-and-white photographic prints, transparencies, and film negatives. Project files include research, correspondence, and print lists as well as preparatory and promotion material related to exhibitions of her images and production of her book.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The River of No Return Collection consists of photographs created by Laura McPhee that document sites and persons in the Sawtooth Valley of Idaho, 2003-2008, related to her work as the initial Artist in Residence in the area underwritten by the Alturas Foundation, as well as project files, 2003-2014, which contain material related imagery and products from the project, including a solo exhibition at the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, in 2006, and her book, River of No Return (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 2009). Photographic material in the collection consists of approximately 2000 items including color and black-and-white photographic prints, transparencies, and film negatives. Project files include research, correspondence, and print lists as well as the artist's maquette for River of No Return and multiple dummies reflecting the design evolution of the book. The collection also includes preparatory and promotional material related to exhibitions of her images.

Exterior images document sites in the Sawtooth Valley in the Sawtooth National Recreation Area of the Sawtooth National Forest, which encompasses portions of Blaine County and Custer County in central Idaho as well as the communities of Custer and Stanley.

Landscape photographs include views of the White Cloud Peaks as well as bodies of water and their surrounding areas including Alturas Lake, Champion Creek, Fisher Creek, Fourth of July Creek, Little Redfish Lake, Lost Creek, Pettit Lake, Redfish Lake Creek, Salmon River (also known as the River of No Return), Stanley Lake, and Valley Creek.
Ranches represented in imagery include Fourth of July Creek Ranch, H-Hook Ranch, Laverty Ranch, and Morgan Williams Ranch.

Enterprises documented in photographs include the Sawtooth Fish Hatchery operated by the Idaho Department of Fish and Game near Stanley, the Grouse Creek Mine operated by the Hecla Mining Company in Custer, the Three Rivers Stone Quarry in Clayton, and deserted mines in Custer including the Bayhorse Lead-Silver Mine and the Clayton Silver Mine.

Discrete groups of images document forest fires in the region as well as fishing and hunting activities. The latter include the butchering of a harvested elk and examples of illegally harvested mammals, birds, and fish confiscated by the Idaho Department of Fish and Game.

Individuals with formal and informal portraits in the collection include Mark Anderson, Isaac Babcock, Robert G. Griswold, Mattie Griswold, Judy Ann Hall-Griswold, Matt Humphreys, Isobel McPhee, Gamaniel Tacza, Tom Thurber, and Rob Trahant.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Laura McPhee on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Photographic Material, 2003-2008. II. Project Files, 2003-2014.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Laura McPhee Photographs of the American West (WA Photos Folio 152). Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Laura McPhee (born 1958)
bioghist.p: Laura McPhee is a photographer. Born in New York City and raised in central New Jersey, she earned a Bachelor of Arts in Art History from Princeton University in 1980, and a Master of Fine Arts in Photography from the Rhode Island School of Design in 1986. Her work, which ranges from portrait to landscape to still life, is widely exhibited nationally and internationally. She has been a member of the faculty at the Massachusetts College of Art and Design since 1986 and lives in Brookline, Massachusetts.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Laura McPhee River of No Return Collection. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

subject.part: Bodies of water -- Idaho -- Sawtooth Valley -- Pictorial works

geogname.part: Sawtooth National Forest (Idaho and Utah) -- Pictorial works
Sawtooth National Recreation Area (Idaho) -- Pictorial works

corpname.part: Idaho. Fish and Game Department -- Pictorial works
Museum of Fine Arts, Boston. -- Exhibitions

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into four subseries: Oversize Prints, Work Prints, Color Transparencies, and Negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic material created by Laura McPhee that documents sites and individuals in the Sawtooth Valley, Idaho, 2003-2008. Material includes oversize color photographic prints, color and black-and-white photographic work prints, transparencies, and film negatives, 2003-2008.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three headings: Chromogenic Color Prints, 51 x 61 cm, Inkjet prints, 44 x 56 cm, and Chromogenic Color Prints, 28 x 35.5 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This material consists of chromogenic color prints on Kodak Professional Endura paper, 28 x 35.5 cm, which include includes incomplete sets of images published in Laura McPhee, River of No Return (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 2009) as well as a set of images that did not appear in the work.
Each folder in this component houses a single print except where noted.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three headings: Book Proof Set, Book Proof Set, and Proofs Not Used in Book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lack prints for images published on pages 23, 71, and 87.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arrangement of prints mirrors the sequence that the images appeared in the book, Laura McPhee, River of No Return .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folders with two prints include images published on pages 11, 41, 84, and 99 of the book. Lack prints for images published on pages 13, 15, 16, 17, 21, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 29, 34, 47, 53, 67, 79, 82, 87, 97, 123, 125, and 127.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arrangement of prints mirrors the sequence that the images appeared in the book, Laura McPhee, River of No Return .

did.unittitle: Illegally Kept Snake River Chinook Salmon with Freezer Burn, Custer County, Idaho , 2005 (Page 85, Negative Number 658)
Lodgepole Pines Killed by Beetles, Nip and Tuck Road, Custer County, Idaho , 2005 (Page 89, Negative Number 2106)
Smoke from a Wildfire Ignited by Sparks from a Burn Barrel, Champion Creek, Custer County, Idaho , 2005 (Page 91, Negative Number 3687)
Smoke Above Fisher Creek, Valley Road Wildfire, 40,838 Acres Burned, Custer County, Idaho , 2005 (Page 92, Negative Number 3703)
Burned Timber, Fourth of July Creek, Valley Road Wildfire, Custer County, Idaho , 2005 (Page 93, Negative Number 3676)
Understory Flareups, Fourth of July Creek, Valley Road Wildfire, Custer County, Idaho , 2005 (Page 95, Negative Number 3698)
Sawtooth Range, Custer County, Idaho , 2004 (Page 99, Negative Number 9641)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 photographic prints.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically under headings for the year of image capture and then sequentially by negative number.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verso of print identified as printed from Negative Number 2124, but it depicts a varient image made from a non-extant negative.

did.unittitle: Images without an Image Capture Date

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verso of print identified as printed from Negative Number 6937, a non-extant negative.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Work prints for all negatives created by Laura McPhee during her residency in the Sawtooth Valley, Idaho, 2003-2008.
Each folder houses a single print except where noted.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged under two headings: Chromogenic Color Prints and Black-and-white Prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chromogenic color prints on Kodak Professional Endura paper, 20.2 x 25.1 cm.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically under headings for the year of image capture and then sequentially by negative number.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically under headings for the year of image capture and then sequentially by negative number followed by undated images without negative numbers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Twenty 35-millimeter color transparencies in plastic mounts and housed in a single sheet that reproduce images created by Laura McPhee during her residency in the Sawtooth Valley, Idaho, 2003-2008.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives for images created by Laura McPhee during her residency in the Sawtooth Valley, Idaho, 2003-2008.
Each folder houses a single negative.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Color Film Negatives and Black and White Negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 20.2 x 25.3 cm.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically under headings for the year of image capture and then sequentially by negative number.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black-and-white film negatives, 20.2 x 25.4 cm, on Kodak Tri-X Pan Professional 4164 film.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically under headings for the year of image capture and then sequentially by negative number followed by undated images without negative numbers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Project files include research, correspondence, and print lists as well as preparatory and promotion material related to exhibitions of her images, her book River of No Return , and other works using her images. There is rich documentation surrounding an exhibition at the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, in 2006.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into eight subseries: Research, Correspondence, Print Lists, Solo Exhibitions, Curated Exhibitions and Events, Book Production, Book Promotion, and Uses of Images in Publications.
